description: A V8 sandbox escape exists in the InstantiateAsmJs Turbofan builtin (src/builtins/builtins-internal-gen.cc:1644-1686) due to a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) mismatch between the dispatch handle and code object used during a tail call. At the start of the builtin, the dispatch handle is captured from the JSFunction. When asm.js instantiation fails, the builtin falls back to JavaScript by tail-calling the function with CompileLazy; however, the code object for the tail call is loaded via LoadJSFunctionCode(function), which reads from the JSFunction's current dispatch handle at the moment of the tail call, while the old dispatch handle (captured at entry) is still passed as the dispatch_handle argument to TailCallJSCode. An attacker with sandbox access can exploit this by swapping the JSFunction's dispatch handle mid-builtin to one pointing to a function with a different formal parameter count: the invoked code will then be set up with the wrong parameter count, corrupting the stack frame and enabling a sandbox escape. Reliable, deterministic exploitation is possible without a race condition by defining a __single_function__ property accessor on WebAssembly.Instance.prototype; this accessor is invoked synchronously inside AsmJs::InstantiateAsmWasm (src/asmjs/asm-js.cc:444) via Object::GetProperty, providing a precise callback window to perform the dispatch handle swap before the tail call executes.

fixes.description: Local patch generated from V8 commit c82024befbfe8458e0482f2f24c9f69361b56e75 to make validation and patched-image builds independent of remote Gerrit fetches.
fixes.evidence_text: c82024befbfe8458e0482f2f24c9f69361b56e75 [asm.js] Fix double read of dispatch handle
